rounds.agent_message: Seed round copied from the highest existing source round.

Implemented the round output in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p84/optimization_rounds/round1/output_model_new.py), updated the round path callsites in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py), and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p84/optimization_rounds/round1/case.txt). The module now exposes the round constants the harness expects, caches the device weight tensor across calls via `_get_cached_weight`, launches with `num_warps=4`, and lowers a kernel whose generated MLIR contains `mfma`. Validation: `python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py` passed with `2 passed in 3.25s`.

Implemented the round-2 split-K path in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p84/optimization_rounds/round2/output_model_new.py): the main kernel now launches with `SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2`, accumulates partial fp32 results into a shared fp32 workspace via `S.amdgpu.buffer_atomic_add_f32`, and a second kernel remaps that workspace back to NCHW bf16 output. The MFMA path remains present in `fused_kernel`, and the model caches the device weight tensor plus a reusable workspace buffer so `forward()` does not build descriptor-like device metadata on each call. I also updated the required callsites in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py), and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/path/to/case.txt) with the current round directory. Validation passed: ```bash python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py ``` `2 passed`
